required_contains.text: The create-a-subnet guide describes burn cost as dynamic recycled TAO that doubles when a subnet is
created and then lowers gradually over time
The lock-reduction interval hyperparameter sets the block span over which that elevated cost halves,
so decay pacing is network-wide rather than stored on an existing netuid

forbidden_contains.text: Burn cost is not a continuous demand estimate. Each subnet creation records the paid amount as the
new lock baseline, and the cost is then read as that last lock doubled, reduced linearly by the
number of blocks elapsed since the last creation. After one lock-reduction interval the cost returns
to the previous lock value and keeps decaying toward the network minimum lock, where it is clamped,
so an idle stretch lets the cost fall back while a fresh creation steps it up again
